# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.

Prevent monitoring misconfiguration.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3' # Specify the version of docker-compose file format 
services:   # Define a service for each component (Elasticsearch, Logstash and Kibana) in ELK Stack with their respective configurations   
     elasticsearch:     
       image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.9.2       
       environment:  # Set the required env variables  
         - node.name=master-node1         
         - cluster.initial_master_nodes=master-node1, master-node2     
         - bootstrap.memory_lock=true    
         - ES_JAVA_MEMORY=-Xms500m       # Set the Java memory allocation for Elasticsearch  
        network: elkstacknet    # Define a custom name (elkstacknet) to use as our Docker Network 
      logstash:    
         image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash-oss      
         environment:         
           - LOGSTASH_JAVA_MEMORY="-Xms1g"   # Set the Java memory allocation for LogStash   
        network: elkstacknet    
      kibana: 
        image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana-oss      
         environment:         
           - ELASTIC_JAVA_MEMORY="-Xms1g"   # Set the Java memory allocation for KIBANA   
     apm:     
        image: docker.elastic.co/apm/apm-server           
       network: elkstacknet 
volumes:          # Define a volume to persist data across container restarts (Elasticsearch, Logstash and APM)  
 elastic_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data     logstash_pipeline:/etc/logstash    apm_data:/var/lib/apm 
networks:          # Define a network for communication between components (ELK Stack, LogStash and Kibana)  
 elkstacknet:      # Custom name to use as our Docker Network. This is the same used in services definition   
     driver: bridge       # Use 'bridge' mode if you want your containers have a private network stack
